---
title: "ASE Data Pipeline Overview"
format: html
---

---

# Datsase Download

Since the option `prefer_scenes_with_max_snippets` is true per default, this will list all scenes sorted by the number of snippets they contain (descending). The `-n 5` flag limits the output to the top 5 scenes.
```{python}
!python -m oracle_rri.data.downloader -m list -c efm_eval -n 5
```

This will download the top 5 scenes with the most snippets, but limit each scene to a maximum of 10 snippets. `-c efm_eval` specifies the configuration to use (here: EFM evaluation set).
```sh
python -m oracle_rri.data.downloader -m download -c efm_eval -n 5 --ms 10
```

This will download the top 5 scenes with the most snippets, using the default configuration (here: EFM training set).
```sh
python -m oracle_rri.data.downloader -m download -c efm -n 5
```


# Dataset config & auto-discovery

The `ASEDatasetConfig` provides several options for configuring the dataset loading process. See the docstring for details.

- **Auto tar resolution**: if `tar_urls` is empty, shards are globbed under resolved ATEK root; if `scene_ids` are provided, only those subdirs are used.
- **Mesh resolution**: when `load_meshes=True`, meshes are loaded from `.data/ase_meshes/scene_ply_{scene}.ply` (or inferred from `scene_ids`).


```{python}
from oracle_rri.data.dataset import ASEDatasetConfig


cfg = ASEDatasetConfig(verbose=True)
cfg.inspect()

ds = cfg.setup_target()
```

The dataset object `ds` yields samples of type "TyppedASESample", this dataclass stores the complex flat-dict from the `AtekWDSDataset` in `ds.flat` and provides typed views via properties.

```{python}
sample = next(iter(ds))
sample.rich_summary(show_only_sample=["efm_gt"])
```

---

# Typed views

```{python}
print(
    sample.camera_rgb,  # CameraView
    # type(sample.camera_slam_left),  # CameraView
    # type(sample.camera_slam_right),  # CameraView
    # # sample.camera_rgb_depth if "mfcd#camera-rgb-depth+images" in sample.flat else None
    # type(sample.trajectory),  # TrajectoryView
    # type(sample.semidense),  # SemiDenseView
    # type(sample.gt),  # GTView (obb2/obb3/efm_gt/rri_targets)
    # type(sample.mesh),  # trimesh.Trimesh or None
)
```

- **CameraView**: images `(F,C,H,W)`, intrinsics, T_device_camera; uses `PoseTW` convention.
- **TrajectoryView**: `ts_world_device` `(F,3,4)`, timestamps, gravity.
- **SemiDenseView**: per-frame SLAM points + stds + volume bounds.
- **GTView**: parses `obb3_gt`, `obb2_gt`, `scores`, `efm_gt`, `rri_targets`; preserves raw.

---

# Mesh handling

- Mesh loaded once per scene (cached if `cache_meshes=True`).
- Mesh shape hints in repr: `{'verts': N, 'faces': M}`.

---
